title: Canadian food prices relative to wages
description: Monthly wage-based pressure differs from household-income affordability because wages cover employed workers while disposable income includes other income and transfers.
plainLanguageSummary: Monthly wage-based pressure differs from household-income affordability because wages cover employed workers while disposable income includes other income and transfers.
howToRead: Compare direction, turning points, and sustained divergences. Use the tooltip for exact values and observation dates; visual alignment alone does not establish causation.

calculation.formula: Published indices and explicitly labelled derived changes; no interpolation across native frequencies.
calculation.explanation: Monthly wage-based pressure differs from household-income affordability because wages cover employed workers while disposable income includes other income and transfers.
calculation.example: A value at date t uses only observations dated t or earlier unless the chart explicitly labels a visual shift.

patternsToWatch: Turning points or divergences that repeat across historical episodes
limitations: Monthly wage-based pressure differs from household-income affordability because wages cover employed workers while disposable income includes other income and transfers.
